key_points: Threat: HIGH. Iranian drones and missiles continue targeting energy infrastructure and military facilities, particularly in the Eastern Province.
Trend: INCREASING. If Iranian proxies such as the Houthis enter the conflict, Saudi Arabia may face attacks from both the north and south simultaneously.
Air Travel: RUH, JED, DMM, and HOF all OPEN with partial operations. Jeddah remains one of the most viable aviation exit hubs for the region.
Ground Travel: Land crossings remain operational but congestion is increasing due to regional evacuation traffic.

situation_assessment.assessment: HIGH. Iranian drones and missiles continue targeting energy infrastructure and military facilities, particularly in the Eastern Province.
INCREASING. If Iranian proxies such as the Houthis enter the conflict, Saudi Arabia may face attacks from both the north and south simultaneously.
Strategic energy infrastructure, US diplomatic and military presence, and proximity to Houthi missile launch areas in Yemen.
Continued interception of drones and missiles. Security posture across military installations and diplomatic facilities remains elevated.
Missile and drone threats likely to continue. Energy infrastructure and military facilities remain primary targets.
If Houthis enter the conflict, missile threats could extend deeper into Saudi territory, including Jeddah and Riyadh.
RUH, JED, DMM, and HOF all OPEN with partial operations. Jeddah remains one of the most viable aviation exit hubs for the region.
Land crossings remain operational but congestion is increasing due to regional evacuation traffic.

airports.name: King Khalid International
airports.notes: Shelter-in-place lifts in waves; diplomatic departures only

airports.name: King Fahd International
airports.notes: Eastern Province rockets forcing runway inspections before dawn ops

airports.name: King Abdulaziz International
airports.notes: West-coast hub; MOD priority lane active

borders.name: King Fahd Causeway (Bahrain)
borders.notes: Limited to force protection convoys during unrest

borders.name: Al Khafji (Kuwait)
borders.notes: Throughput steady; used for coalition sustainment

borders.name: Al Hadithah / Mudawwarah (Jordan)
borders.notes: Humanitarian lane plus ammo resupply convoys

borders.name: Al Salmi / Nuwaiseeb (Kuwait)
borders.notes: Northern exit corridor; monitor for convoy prioritization

borders.name: Yemen crossings (all)
borders.notes: All crossings closed; active conflict both sides of border

domestic_unrest.notes: Zero reports of protests, demonstrations, or instability. Eastern Province Shia population calm — economic interdependence self-restraining. Official messaging emphasizes national unity. Contrast: protests reported in Pakistan, Iran.

energy_infra.notes: Ras Tanura refinery (550,000 bpd) shut for assessment; local supply unaffected. Abqaiq OPERATIONAL. East-West Pipeline active, Aramco rerouting exports via Yanbu/Red Sea. No forced production cuts. Output >10M bpd.

evac_routes.label: Riyadh → Amman
evac_routes.notes: Jordan FIR open for commercial traffic as of 03 MAR. Fuel stops sparse north of Tabuk — top off in Ha'il or Tabuk.

evac_routes.label: Riyadh → Kuwait City
evac_routes.notes: 6 US KIA in Kuwait as of 03 MAR. Shortest route from Riyadh; crossing currently open.

evac_routes.label: → Yemen (any crossing)
evac_routes.notes: All Yemen crossings CLOSED as of 03 MAR. Active multi-front conflict; no consular support available.

export_logistics.notes: Aramco rerouting crude via East-West Pipeline (Abqaiq→Yanbu, 5-7M bpd) to Red Sea. Buyers of Arab Light instructed to load at Yanbu. Hormuz bypass active.

iw_matrix.notes: RUH/DMM: obstructed. JED: open. US Consulate shelter-in-place issued for Dhahran.
Two drones struck US Embassy Riyadh Mar 3 06:00Z. Eastern Province on full alert. Saudi armed forces at maximum readiness.
Ras Tanura refinery (550,000 bpd) shut for assessment. Abqaiq OPERATIONAL. East-West Pipeline active — Aramco rerouting via Yanbu.
Aramco bypassing Hormuz via East-West Pipeline to Yanbu/Red Sea. Production >10M bpd with no forced cuts.
US Embassy Riyadh struck (two drones, Mar 3). US Consulate Dhahran: imminent threat warning issued.
Zero protests or instability. Public life continues normally. Eastern Province Shia population calm.
Al Wadiah / Sharurah border area under pressure from Yemen side. Houthis signaling Red Sea escalation.
